message:
fix: use regex for MSW API catch-all to match multi-segment paths (#9814)

The MSW catch-all handler used '/api/*' which in MSW v2 only matches
single-segment paths like '/api/foo'. Enterprise compliance dashboards
fetch multi-segment paths like '/api/compliance/nist/families' which
slipped through the catch-all, hit Netlify's SPA fallback, and received
index.html (200 OK, text/html) instead of JSON — causing the
"Unexpected token '<'" parse error on every enterprise dashboard.

Changed the catch-all to a regex (/^\/api\//) that matches all paths
under /api/ regardless of depth. Also added a safeJson() utility for
defense-in-depth content-type validation.

Fixes #9797

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -677,6 +677,27 @@ export const api = new ApiClient()
  *
  * Existing callers only need to change `fetch(url, init)` -> `authFetch(url, init)`.
  */
+/**
+ * Safely parse a Response as JSON, guarding against HTML responses.
+ *
+ * On Netlify, unmatched API routes fall through to the SPA catch-all which
+ * returns index.html (200 OK, text/html). Calling `.json()` on that response
+ * throws "Unexpected token '<'" (#9797). This helper checks the Content-Type
+ * header first and throws a descriptive error instead of a cryptic parse error.
+ *
+ * Usage:
+ *   const data = await safeJson<MyType>(response)
+ */
+export async function safeJson<T = unknown>(response: Response): Promise<T> {
+  const contentType = response.headers.get('content-type') || ''
+  if (!contentType.includes('application/json')) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Expected JSON response but received ${contentType || 'unknown content-type'} (status ${response.status})`,
+    )
+  }
+  return response.json() as Promise<T>
+}
+
 export function authFetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {
   const token = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY_TOKEN)
   const headers = new Headers(init?.headers)
